issue_title: search: read Government IDs from queries

issue_body: Watchman Version: `v0.29.1` **What were you trying to do?** When calling the `search` endpoint, we are seeing an empty string response which is leading to errors when the search api tries to map the response. **What did you expect to see?** I expect to see responses back from the search endpoint, or an empty Search object **What did you see?** The screenshot below is of the generated open api spec code. The localVarResponse.body() is just an empty string. When `this.memberVarObjectMapper.readValue((String)localVarResponse.body(), new TypeReference() {})` is evaluated we get the following error: ``` Exception in thread "main" java.lang.RuntimeException: java.lang.RuntimeException: java.lang.RuntimeException: java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException: com.current.watchman.ApiException: com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.MismatchedInputException: No content to map due to end-of-input at [Source: REDACTED (`StreamReadFeature.INCLUDE_SOURCE_IN_LOCATION` disabled); line: 1] at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture.reportGet(CompletableFuture.java:396) at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture.get(CompletableFuture.java:2073) at com.current.persona.scripts.WatchmanScript.lambda$main$bd191920$1(WatchmanScript.java:39) at io.vavr.control.Try.of(Try.java:75) at com.current.persona.scripts.WatchmanScript.lambda$main$3(WatchmanScript.java:22) at java.base/java.lang.Iterable.forEach(Iterable.java:75) at com.current.persona.scripts.WatchmanScript.lambda$main$4(WatchmanScript.java:21) at com.current.core.ThrowingConsumer.accept(ThrowingConsumer.java:11) ... 6 more Caused by: com.current.watchman.ApiException: com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.MismatchedInputException: No content to map due to end-of-input at [Source: REDACTED (`StreamReadFeature.INCLUDE_SOURCE_IN_LOCATION` disabled); line: 1] at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.MismatchedInputException.from(MismatchedInputException.java:59) at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper._initForReading(ObjectMapper.java:4996) at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper._readMapAndClose(ObjectMapper.java:4898) at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper.readValue(ObjectMapper.java:3848) at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper.readValue(ObjectMapper.java:3831) at com.current.watchman.client.WatchmanApi.lambda$search$22(WatchmanApi.java:1184) ... 8 more ``` **How can we reproduce the problem?** You can reproduce by starting the container ``` docker run -p 8084:8084 -p 9094:9094 moov/watchman:latest # or more specifically docker run -p 8084:8084 -p 9094:9094 moov/watchman:v0.30.0 And then do watch -n1 'curl "localhost:8084/search?q=WADDANI&limit=1"' ``` You’ll intermittently get empty responses We use `v0.29.1` in our production environment, but we are able to reproduce using the latest version too. issue_comments:
@adamdecaf Im not sure the fix worked

I pulled that and did the same thing

`docker run -p 8084:8084 -p 9094:9094 moov/watchman:v0.31.2`
And then did

`bash -c 'ECHO=false; watchman_searches=0; while true; do OUTPUT=$(curl -s "localhost:8084/search?q=WADDANI&limit=1"); if [[ $ECHO == "true" ]]; then echo $OUTPUT; fi; ((watchman_searches++)); echo "Successfully searched via watchman [$watchman_searches] times"; if [[ -z $OUTPUT ]]; then echo "Watchman search did not return results when it shouuld have"; break; fi; done'`

And it runs on average < 10 times.

@haleyhartin7, there's [a chat about this](https://moov-io.slack.com/archives/CFUCEBGH2/p1736630271112269) in the [Moov Slack](https://slack.moov.io/).

Also, thank you for identifying the last working version. You're a life-saver!! 🛟

You're right - there was more needed to fix this. NaN snuck out and was causing havoc during JSON marshal. 

https://github.com/moov-io/watchman/commit/d81722223fda0d2503c8dee93baba20c743fbd2b
For curiosity, do we know if the NaN was from an actual error in the list? Or just an edge case that wasn't covered?

It was an edge case in the similarity scoring, when an empty string was compared against.  Could be from parsing the list incorrectly, but not due to list retrieval/parse error. 

https://github.com/moov-io/watchman/commit/d81722223fda0d2503c8dee93baba20c743fbd2b#diff-09ec461c3bfc3f8ff11e8a50789e8cb5c56cc816f9fb3c9c646e62bf3b5c06c9R430

issue_title: Downloaded sanction list text/csv files are not deleted after they are not needed any more
issue_body:
The downloader package [here](https://github.com/moov-io/watchman/blob/master/pkg%2Fdownload%2Fclient.go#L70) creates a new temporary directory (with random directory name) every time the sanction lists are refreshed. (Standard every twelve hours) 

The file content is parsed immediately after the download and then abandoned.

This fills up the `/tmp` folder over time. A docker container running 24/7 will waste a couple of GB of physical storage every week of running. It is not much but noticeable over time.

Is there a reason why the temp files are not deleted immediately after they are not used any more. One could even use a suitable in memory buffer instead of a physical temporary file to avoid this in the first place!
issue_comments: No real reason why it's not deleted yet. We can remove it after parsing.

issue_body:
Currently, this is logging all search queries which means sensitive customer information (PII) is being stored in logs.

There are some options:
- Don’t log search queries at all
- Configure logging of search queries using an env variable (on/off)
- Change the logging of search queries to `trace` so that the current `LOG_LEVEL` configuration prevents them from getting logged.
issue_comments: Thanks for the call out. Not sure how that snuck through all these years.

issue_body:
>  I am assuming in this Moov library we are using Jaro winkler algorithm. If I try the same in Python, I am getting a match rate of 53.72% where as in Watchman it is showing as 100%. This is what I tried:

```python
# pip install jaro-winkler
import jaro
jaro.jaro_winkler_metric(u"BELLETECH CORP", u"11420 CORP.")

```

issue_title: search: filtering by the country shouldn't be limited
issue_body:
<!-- Please fill out the following questions, thanks! -->

Watchman Version: `v0.15.1`

**What were you trying to do?**
When filtering on a country it can sometimes chop off the results in an unexpected way. Imagine having 101 search results for "Columbia" and our `?limit=100`, but the SDN we expect to find is the 101st entity. Filtering out the first country results means we won't return the expected entity. 

**What did you expect to see?**
Including a filter like `?country=Colombia` should filter results regardless of the query limit.

issue_title: webui: Country "All" is passed over to the search endpoint
issue_body:
<!-- Please fill out the following questions, thanks! -->

Watchman Version: `v0.15.1`

**What were you trying to do?**
In the webui when Country is set to `All` that value is passed to `GET /search?country=All` which doesn't work as expected. With other query params (e.g. sdnType) the "All" value isn't passed through to the endpoint.

**What did you expect to see?**
Searching across all countries shouldn't apply a filter in the results.

issue_title: Bug: When looping through the watches in spawnResearching, if a watch customer or company is does not have a SDN match, the body is not initialized and throws a panic in callWebhook which is should not call.
issue_body:
<!-- Please fill out the following questions, thanks! -->

Watchman Version: `v0.14.3`

**What were you trying to do?**
I added a customer watch at `/ofac/customers/watch?name=Taylor Green`, when in the spawnResearching method and looping through the for loop (line 49) it enters a switch statement where it looks at customerID, customerName, companyID and companyName. If the current watch record does not get to where the body is initialized (not found in SDNs), it continues down to callWebhook with a nil body object and causes a panic.

Actually will need to check if body is nil after a switch is complete.

**What did you expect to see?**
Move on to the next watch customer or company.


**What did you see?**
panic


**How can we reproduce the problem?**
see above.
issue_comments:
~~Was this different than https://github.com/moov-io/watchman/pull/291 ? I'm going to look at this issue.~~

Fixed in https://github.com/moov-io/watchman/pull/297

`line 59: webhook.go`

@swhyte-rt Thanks. That's an oversight from us. I've fixed the calling code: https://github.com/moov-io/watchman/pull/290

issue_title: Potential collision and risk from indirect dependence "github.com/gotestyourself/gotestyourself"
issue_body:
## Background
Repo `moov-io/watchman` used the old path to import `gotestyourself` indirectly.
This caused that `github.com/gotestyourself/gotestyourself` and `gotest.tools` coexist in this repo：
https://github.com/moov-io/watchman/blob/master/go.mod （Line 13 & 25）
```
github.com/gotestyourself/gotestyourself v2.2.0+incompatible // indirect
gotest.tools v2.2.0+incompatible // indirect 
```

That’s because the `gotestyourself` has already renamed it’s import path from "github.com/gotestyourself/gotestyourself" to "gotest.tools". When you use the old path "github.com/gotestyourself/gotestyourself" to import the `gotestyourself`, will reintroduces `gotestyourself` through the import statements "import gotest.tools" in the go source file of `gotestyourself`.

https://github.com/gotestyourself/gotest.tools/blob/v2.2.0/fs/example_test.go#L8 
```
package fs_test
import (
	…
	"gotest.tools/assert"
	"gotest.tools/assert/cmp"
	"gotest.tools/fs"
	"gotest.tools/golden"
)
```

"github.com/gotestyourself/gotestyourself" and "gotest.tools" are the same repos. This will work in isolation, bring about potential risks and problems. 


## Solution
Add replace statement in the go.mod file:
```
replace github.com/gotestyourself/gotestyourself => gotest.tools v2.3.0
```
Then clean the go.mod.
issue_comments: @adamdecaf  Could you help me review this issue? Thx :p
Sure thing @KateGo520 I think we should update the gotest.tools dependency. I think we had this with another project.

Since that dependency is transitive I ran `go get -u ./...` and the `go.sum` seems to be upgraded to their `/v3` release. Does that fix your issue?

I pushed a `upgrade-deps-2020-08-12` branch. Can you try that? 

If all looks good I can merge https://github.com/moov-io/watchman/pull/285
@adamdecaf  Thank you for your reply. I see the commit. I think it looks good. Thank you for your contribution!
Merged. Can you let me know if Watchman isn't working for you after that update?

issue_title: ofac: SDN comments contains improper quotes
issue_body: The sdn_comments.csv file has improperly escaped quotes. Here's the problem record: ``` 28264,"hone Number 8613314257947; alt. Phone Number 8618004121000; Identification Number 210302198701102136 (China); a.k.a. "blackjack1987"; a.k.a. "khaleesi"; Linked To: LAZARUS GROUP." ``` The quotes around"blackjack1987" and "khaleesi" should be escaped ( \" ) . I've emailed OFAC and am awaiting their response, for now we can probably fix this by removing inner quotes.

issue_title: search: ?q=24 incorrectly matches against partial remarks ID
issue_body:
Reported from @atonks2 

A query like: `?q=24` incorrectly matches against a partial remarks ID from this SDN:

```
23410,"WORLD WATER FISHERIES LIMITED",-0- ,"LIBYA3",-0- ,-0- ,-0- ,-0- ,-0- ,-0- ,-0- ,"D-U-N-S Number 56-558-7594; V.A.T. Number MT15388917 (Malta); Trade License No. C 24129 (Malta); Company Number 4220856; Linked To: DEBONO, Darren."
```

The extracted remarks ID from this SDN record is `C 24129`, which is correct. Our current logic for when a space exists is to just `strings.Contains` the query and parsed ID. This logic needs to change such that "all the numeric parts have to exactly match". 

- `?q=24129` should return this SDN
- `?q=C+24129` should return this SDN
- `?q=24` should **not** return this SDN

issue_title: sdnType: special case 'entity' to return null SDN Type records
issue_body:
When providing the `&sdnType=entity` filter no results are returned. This is a bit confusing as "not individuals" are called entities from OFAC, so we need to handle them the same.

From OFAC: 

> "Entity would be the category for company or organization. You can type in the name in the name search box to perform the search via the link to this search engine: https://sanctionssearch.ofac.treas.gov/  Thank you."

issue_title: search: ignore stop words in matching
issue_body:
Stop words are typically useless words in sentences and are also the most frequent. They're excluded because the verb, noun, adjectives, etc contain information which the speaker weights more heavily over these grammatical nuances. 

There's a Go library which looks to be setup for removing and ranking strings according to a language's common stop words. We'd need to add support for Jaro Winkler. 

https://github.com/bbalet/stopwords / [LevenshteinDistance](https://godoc.org/github.com/bbalet/stopwords#LevenshteinDistance)
issue_comments: If each list contains country/language information we should use that to guess the language, because otherwise we'll have to guess the language another way.
Daniel brought up a point where the default stop words list should contain the most common words from several languages (think: of, the, and) with a config to override the default set.

issue_title: latest ofac version requires an invalid go4.org pseudo-version
issue_body:
In commit 9844e58018f379b5bc948813edba6ff91df80449, the go4.org module requirement was updated to an invalid pseudo-version. In commit 6e70f243f0c77aab203053a0d91edfba36a534e1, a replace directive was added with the correct pseudo-version.

A problem is that the replace directive only applies when this is the main module, so it doesn't help other projects that try to require and use this module.

You can reproduce by doing:

```
$ go version
go version go1.13.4 darwin/amd64
$ cd $(mktemp -d)
$ go mod init example.com/m
go: creating new go.mod: module example.com/m
$ go get github.com/moov-io/ofac@latest
go: finding github.com/moov-io/ofac v0.11.1
go: downloading github.com/moov-io/ofac v0.11.1
go: extracting github.com/moov-io/ofac v0.11.1
go get: github.com/moov-io/ofac@v0.11.1 requires
	go4.org@v0.0.0-20190430205326-94abd6928b1d: invalid pseudo-version: does not match version-control timestamp (2019-03-13T08:23:47Z)
```

The right fix would be to remove the replace directive and change the required version to be valid.
issue_comments: Thanks for bringing this up. I've released an RC version which should fix this, but I plan for a proper v0.12.0 release early this week.

issue_body: Using this service (or the example available at https://moov.io/ofac/) does not match results from the treasury's official SDN search at https://sanctionssearch.ofac.treas.gov/. This service returns way too many unrelated results with a high match value. For example, searching "George Bush" returns an 89% match to "George Habbash", 88% match to "George Haswani", 84% match to "George Chiweshe": However, searching the same name with an 80% fuzzy match threshold on the Treasury's own OFAC search website yields no results: I love the open source fintech toolbox. Unfortunately these tools are not high enough quality to use in production at a financial institution today. I look forward to helping to improve the library. Thanks!
issue_comments: @brobits Thanks for the report. I'm not quite sure how OFAC's site performs similarity calculations, but I [opened a pull request](https://github.com/moov-io/ofac/pull/116) to help address this. Any advice you have would be much appreciated!

Hi, @adamdecaf! Thanks for taking a look at this. I'm not sure what the Treasury uses for their fuzzy logic matching, but I found a thread discussing a few algorithms here: https://www.bankersonline.com/forum/ubbthreads.php/topics/2147612/ofac-fuzzy-logic-recommendations

They mention the Jaro-Winkler algorithm which I believe this library also uses (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaro%E2%80%93Winkler_distance). They also mention the Soundex (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soundex) algorithm. I assume the Treasury also uses Jaro-Winkler?

OFAC uses jaro-winkler, yes. I picked it over Levenshtein and Jaro because of the improvements it provides. 

We could support soundex (probably by setting `&algorithm=soundex` in the URL), but I'd like to harden the current usage of jaro-winkler.
Awesome! Thanks for the insight, no worries about alternative implementations provided results are similar enough to the Treasury. Thanks again for taking a look at this so quickly.
v0.10.0 will be released today with this included

issue_title: OFAC service can fail on start-up
issue_body:
If the OFAC service has a failure getting the sanctions list it will exit with a failure.  I'm not sure this is the most ideal behavior.

Maybe it should keep trying?  Maybe it can try from some back up locations?  Not sure the best behavior but crashing does not seem ideal.

(Note: we are not currently persisting the DB.  So maybe this is a problem only on initial run when db is created?)
issue_comments: Yea. I've noticed it too, so let's retry the download. I've thought about an env variable to specify a directory for downloads (rather than a temporary directory) which could be persisted, but haven't written that code.

issue_title: search: handle all address query parameters
issue_body:
We need to handle all search params (right now it's just req.Address). This needs to be done so we search across the metadata provided in each OFAC entry.

This includes the following fields/params: .City, .State, .Providence, .Zip, .Country

I assume searching the SDN entry for whatever is in City, State, Providence and Zip (as separate searches?) would be a good start.

I'm not exactly sure how to correlate Country though. Often records are part of the "IRAN" or "CUBA" sanction program, or shows up in the remarks column. 

I realized this when reading over [an OFAC settlement for shipping to Iran](https://www.treasury.gov/resource-center/sanctions/CivPen/Documents/20190327_decker_settlement.pdf).
